100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 2, 1926, the British government faced mounting pressure as strikes continued to ripple across the nation, particularly within the coal industry, where miners demanded better wages and working conditions. In Germany, political unrest simmered as the Weimar Republic struggled to maintain stability amid economic turmoil. Meanwhile, in the United States, the film industry buzzed with excitement over the latest silent film releases, showcasing the vibrant cultural scene of the Roaring Twenties.
